Record Class DemoShell

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
com.embabel.template.DemoShell
@ShellComponent record DemoShell(InjectedDemo injectedDemo, com.embabel.agent.core.AgentPlatform agentPlatform) extends Record
Spring Shell commands demonstrating different ways to invoke Embabel agents.

Key Embabel Concept — Execution Modes: Embabel supports three execution modes:

  • Focused mode (demo command below): User code programmatically invokes a specific agent via AgentInvocation. The platform runs the agent's GOAP planner to build and execute a plan toward the requested output type. This is the most common mode in production applications (e.g., invoked from a REST controller or event handler).
  • Closed mode (the built-in x "prompt" command): The platform uses an LLM to classify user input and select the most appropriate registered agent. Only actions within the selected agent are considered.
  • Open mode: The platform selects a goal (not an agent) and builds a custom agent from all available actions across all registered agents to achieve it. Most powerful but least deterministic.

@ShellComponent: A Spring Shell stereotype. Methods annotated with @ShellMethod become interactive CLI commands. Embabel also registers its own dynamic commands (x, execute, chat) for agent invocation.

See Also:
  • AgentInvocation
  • AgentPlatform

  • Constructor Details

    • DemoShell

      DemoShell(InjectedDemo injectedDemo, com.embabel.agent.core.AgentPlatform agentPlatform)

      Parameters:
      injectedDemo - the value for the injectedDemo record component
      agentPlatform - the value for the agentPlatform record component

  • Method Details

    • demo

      @ShellMethod("Demo") String demo()
      Demonstrates Focused mode agent invocation — the most common pattern in production applications.

      AgentInvocation.create(AgentPlatform, Class) creates an invocation targeting the goal output type (ReviewedStory.class). The platform finds the agent that can produce this type, runs the GOAP planner, and executes the resulting plan.

      The result implements HasContent, so getContent() returns a formatted string suitable for display.

    • animal

      @ShellMethod("Invent an animal") String animal()
      Demonstrates using Embabel's Ai interface outside of an agent context, via the injected InjectedDemo component.

      This shows that LLM operations are not limited to @Agent classes — any Spring bean can use the Ai interface for ad-hoc LLM calls.

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object).
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
